sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
When enabling MFA (multi-factor authentication), Atlas requests two forms of identification:
your password and one of the following recommended factors:

- **Security Key / Biometrics**

- **Okta Verify Mobile App**

  - OTP

  - Push Notifications

- **Authenticator App** (TOTP (Time-based One-time Password Algorithm))

  You can choose any TOTP (Time-based One-time Password Algorithm) for authentication. We recommend enabling
  cloud backups when possible to avoid being locked out of your account.

  - [Twilio Authy](https://authy.com/)

  - [Google Authenticator](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/1066447)

  - [Microsoft Authenticator](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/mobile-authenticator-app)

- **Email** (use with caution)

- **SMS** (deprecated)

  > **Important:** SMS authentication is deprecated. Only existing SMS users can
  > continue to use this method with their current phone numbers.
  > Atlas does not accept new SMS registrations.

sections.heading: Set Up Backup Multi-Factor Authentication Methods
sections.body:
> **Warning:** Enable a minimum of two methods so that you can still access your
> account if you lose access to one method.

While you can set up one, some, or all of the available methods, we
strongly recommend that you set up at least two methods. When Atlas
requires MFA (multi-factor authentication), it offers you the choice of which method to use. If you
have less than two methods set up, Atlas prompts you to set up
MFA (multi-factor authentication) and a backup method at login.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To use MFA (multi-factor authentication), an [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) must enable it for
their organization. All members of that organization must enable
MFA (multi-factor authentication) for their accounts.

After MFA (multi-factor authentication) is enabled for an organization, Atlas prompts those
organization members who haven't enabled MFA (multi-factor authentication) to set it up on their next
login. If an organization member doesn't enable MFA (multi-factor authentication), they won't be able
to access the organization.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
Install and configure multiple authentication factors.

**Okta:**

Okta Verify Mobile App

Download the Okta Verify app to your
[iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app//okta-verify/id490179405) or
[Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.okta.android.auth) device.

**Webauthn:**

Security Key / Biometric

Install and configure a FIDO2 (Fast Identity Online version 2) compatible device (like a
YubiKey) or operating system feature (like Windows Hello).

> **Note:** FIDO2 (Fast Identity Online version 2) compatibility varies. It depends on your platform
> and browser. Consult [Okta's documentation](https://help.okta.com/en/prod/Content/Topics/Security/mfa-webauthn.htm#WebAuthn2)
> before choosing this method.

**Authapp:**

Authenticator App

Download an Authenticator App (for example, Google Authenticator
or Microsoft Authenticator) to your iOS or Android device.

**Email:**

Email

- Verify the email address you used to sign up for your MongoDB
  account.

- Verify that this email address can receive email from the
  `mongodb.com` domain.

sections.heading: Enable Multi-Factor Authentication
sections.body:
- Click on your name in the upper right corner of the Atlas
  console. A dropdown menu displays.

- Click Manage your MongoDB Account.

- Click Security in the left-side navigation.

* Choose your preferred authentication method.

* Click Set up to the right of your chosen method.

* Follow the procedure for your chosen method:

Okta Verify Mobile App

Complete these steps using the Okta Verify mobile app.

- Open the Okta Verify app on your mobile device.

- Tap the + icon to add an account. The app places
  this icon in the app's menu bar at the top of the screen.

- Scan the bar code displayed in Atlas.

- Click Done.

Security Key / Biometric

Complete these steps using Atlas and your FIDO2 (Fast Identity Online version 2) device.

- On the Set up Security Key/Biometric
  Authenticator page, click Enroll.

- Your configured MFA (multi-factor authentication) device requests verification of your
  identity.

Authenticator App

Complete these steps using the Authenticator App.

- Open the Authenticator App to your mobile device.

- Tap the + icon to add an account. The app places
  this icon in the app's menu bar at the bottom right of the
  screen.

- Tap Scan a QR code.

- Scan the bar code displayed in Atlas.

Enter the six-digit verification code into Atlas.

Email

- Check your email account for a message titled **Verify Your
  Identity** from `mongodb-account@mongodb.com`. Atlas
  sent an email message with a six-digit verification code.

- Open the message to get the code.

- Enter this code into Atlas.

After you set up your first authentication method, repeat the steps
to set up another method.

sections.heading: Remove an Authentication Method
sections.body:
To remove a method from your account:

- Choose a method to remove.

- Click trash-alt **Delete** to the right of that method.

To remove a method, Atlas asks you to authenticate again using
MFA (multi-factor authentication).
